wormhole.audience: explicitly addressed to security researchers and automated tools

entries.mechanism: NTT attestations are evaluated against the CURRENT threshold and CURRENT enabled-transceiver set at execution time, rather than against the (threshold, enabled-set) snapshot that existed when the attestation was recorded. Administrative threshold/transceiver reductions and re-enables are deliberate admin actions.
entries.impact: an attestation recorded under one transceiver/threshold config counting toward quorum under a later config (incl. re-enable of a previously-removed transceiver) — DECLARED NON-ISSUE.
entries.raw_text: NTT attestations are evaluated against live threshold/transceiver config rather than a snapshot taken when the attestation was recorded; admin threshold reductions are deliberate; any fraudulent-attestation scenario requires a quorum of malicious Guardians (out of scope).

entries.mechanism: any impact that assumes control over a quorum of signing keys (Guardians / malicious transceiver quorum) as a precondition.
entries.impact: compromising independent Guardian infra deemed infeasible without evidence; INVALID as a PoC assumption — out of scope.
entries.raw_text: Any impact that assumes control over a quorum of signing keys as a precondition is out of scope; compromising independent Guardian infrastructure is infeasible without supporting evidence.

entries.mechanism: denial-of-service based on linear usage of a rate-limiting mechanism via legitimate transfers.
entries.impact: normal capacity consumption is expected; only disproportionate inflation counts — otherwise non-issue.
entries.raw_text: Denial-of-service based on linear usage of a rate limiting mechanism is expected behavior; only disproportionate inflation of consumed capacity counts as a potential bug.

bitflow.audience: Immunefi PoC-required program; in-repo docs enumerate tested+guaranteed invariants

entries.mechanism: modifying tx args/params, substituting contract addresses, submitting malicious txs against an already-connected wallet.
entries.impact: OUT OF SCOPE per program rules.
entries.raw_text: Malicious interactions with an already-connected wallet (modifying tx args, substituting contract addresses, malicious txs) are out of scope.

entries.mechanism: a finding that requires a token NOT deployed from SPQC38PW542EQJ5M11CR25P7BS1CA6QT4TBXGB3M; the system whitelists tokens and has a mock-unwhitelisted-token for negative testing (unwhitelisted tokens are rejected by design).
entries.impact: OUT OF SCOPE — only tokens from the named deployer are in scope; unwhitelisted-token paths are tested-rejects, not bugs.
entries.raw_text: Tokens deployed from SPQC38PW542EQJ5M11CR25P7BS1CA6QT4TBXGB3M are in scope; other tokens are not.

entries.mechanism: swaps must not mint/burn LP tokens (LP supply unchanged during swap) — fuzzed as checkSwapXForY/YForX invariants (Critical).
entries.impact: a finding merely RE-STATING this invariant without a concrete BREAK is covered by the project's own Critical fuzz property; only a demonstrated violation is novel.
entries.raw_text: LP supply must remain unchanged during swaps. Violation impact: Critical.

entries.mechanism: bin balances change by (input-fees)/output; user balances change exactly by swapped amounts; arithmetic-edge (overflow/underflow/div-zero/boundary) + rounding + 'exploit detection' covered by 276+ unit tests incl. swap-calculations-validation-logic + arithmetic-edge-cases.
entries.impact: plain rounding/slippage/precision/overflow claims without a NET value-extraction proof beyond the tested envelope are covered; only an untested composition that breaks conservation is novel.
entries.raw_text: Input token balance increases by (input - fees); output decreases by output; user balances change by exactly the amounts swapped. Arithmetic edge-cases + rounding fuzzed/tested.

arkadiko.audience: 4.5yr protocol, multiple audits; Audit.md lists fixed findings

entries.mechanism: oracle price signatures re-used; FIXED by storing each signature in a map (buff->bool) and rejecting re-use (CR-01).
entries.impact: replay of a signed price; FIXED/known.
entries.raw_text: CR-01 Signature Replay in Price Oracle - signatures now stored in a map; re-use fails.

entries.mechanism: mint fee could be front-run; FIXED by adding max-mint-fee param to open-vault/update-vault (ME-02).
entries.raw_text: ME-02 Lost Funds due to Fee Front-Running - added max-mint-fee param.

entries.mechanism: auth via tx-sender; FIXED switched to contract-caller; admin via get-dao-owner; wstx-token L55 deliberately keeps tx-sender (ME-03).
entries.impact: phishing/intermediate-contract auth; FIXED/known. wstx-token tx-sender use is BY DESIGN.
entries.raw_text: ME-03 switched tx-sender->contract-caller; wstx-token L55 keeps tx-sender by design.

entries.mechanism: rewards must NOT use fragments-per-token (that var only converts staker amount->fragments for USDA-burn rebasing); rewards distribute across total fragments (ME-01).
entries.raw_text: ME-01 rewards logic should not take fragments-per-token into account.

aave-umbrella.audience: Aave Immunefi $1M; 4 audits (Certora/MixBytes/Ackee/StErMi); in-repo accepted-risk doc + audit acknowledgements

entries.class: exchange-rate inflation / precision loss within bound
entries.mechanism: totalSupply/totalAssets can grow up to 2^256/1e6 by design; soft-capped at 100 (redeploy beyond); violating soft reqs zeroes new rewards (precision loss). totalAssets<=totalSupply hard-enforced (slash reduces totalAssets only).
entries.impact: share-price inflation / reward precision-loss WITHIN these documented bounds = accepted design, NOT a bug.
entries.raw_text: totalAssets<=totalSupply; totalSupply/totalAssets soft-capped 100; precision loss when soft reqs violated.

entries.mechanism: StakeToken accepts ANY ERC20 as underlying asset, not only StataTokenV2; fee-on-transfer / non-standard tokens are incompatible by documented limitation.
entries.impact: findings requiring a fee-on-transfer or non-standard ERC20 underlying = documented limitation / OOS.
entries.raw_text: StakeToken accepts any ERC20; fee-on-transfer incompatible; BGD documents supported asset types.

entries.mechanism: StataTokenV2 accrued rewards are not recorded/awarded when the stataToken is deposited into StakeToken or unwrapped/transferred via the batch helper.
entries.impact: loss of stataToken-side rewards in these flows = acknowledged/documented limitation.
entries.raw_text: stataToken accrued rewards owed are not recorded on unwrap/transfer; acknowledged.

entries.class: rewards not refreshed on all flows
entries.mechanism: by design, reward indexes are not refreshed on every flow (adding new reward tokens is rare vs flow frequency).
entries.impact: stale-reward-index on a flow that didn't refresh = by-design, acknowledged.
entries.raw_text: By design, we didn't want to include refreshing of rewards on all flows.

entries.mechanism: slash and admin functions are onlyOwner; owner is an UmbrellaController with ACL, called only by DAO/strong-multisig (5-9), never an EOA.
entries.impact: msg.sender/admin-trust/centralization findings on slash/config = accepted trust model.
entries.raw_text: owner installs controller with ACL; only DAO/multisig calls slash; msg.sender not useful here.
